title: Forgiveness Can Be Revisited
subtitle: Five affirmations for the non-linear work of forgiving
excerpt: Five affirmations for the person who thought they had forgiven something and then found it back again. Use them when the feeling returns and you need a reminder that this is how the process works.

practice.whenToUse: Use when resentment or hurt that you thought you had processed comes back, and you feel like you have failed at forgiving.

content.text: Five affirmations for the moment when the feeling returns and you think you have gone backwards. You have not gone backwards. This is how the process works.
Forgiveness is a practice, not a single event. I am allowed to return to it.
I have forgiven some of this. That is enough for today.
The feeling that comes back is not failure. It is the next layer.
I am allowed to return to this as many times as it takes.
Each time I choose to put this down, it stays down a little longer.
